seo.title: Seedance 2.0 Fast | Fast Text, Image & Reference Video
seo.description: Use Seedance 2.0 Fast on MaxVideoAI for faster text-to-video, start/end-frame image-to-video, and reference-to-video drafts with audio.

overview: Seedance 2.0 Fast is the quick-iteration tier in the Seedance 2 family on Fal. It keeps the same three workflow types as standard Seedance 2.0, but is better suited to storyboard passes, timing checks, and rapid comparison loops.
pricingNotes: Seedance 2 Fast uses Fal token pricing. MaxVideoAI adds its margin and rounds up to the next cent; Generate shows the final quote before submission.

hero.title: Seedance 2.0 Fast
hero.intro: Seedance 2.0 Fast keeps the same three workflows as the standard tier: text-to-video, image-to-video with an optional end frame, and reference-to-video with image, video, and audio references. Use it when you want shorter iteration loops, faster A/B checks, and a lighter draft tier before you commit to standard Seedance 2.0.
hero.badge: Fast tier · 3 workflows · 480p/720p · Auto or 4-15s · Native audio

technicalOverview.label: Role in the family
technicalOverview.body: Optimized fast variant in the Seedance 2 family on Fal.

technicalOverview.label: Inputs
technicalOverview.body: The same three core Seedance 2 workflows: text-to-video, image-to-video with an optional end frame, and ref2v with image, video, and audio references

technicalOverview.label: Reference limits
technicalOverview.body: Up to 9 image references, 3 video references, and 3 audio references, with 12 total files across the full ref2v run

technicalOverview.label: Audio
technicalOverview.body: Native audio is on by default and can be disabled; Fal states the price is the same either way

technicalOverview.label: Best moment to use it
technicalOverview.body: Early exploration, shot planning, and pre-final comparison rounds

technicalOverview.label: Fal positioning
technicalOverview.body: Fal's public Seedance page groups Fast with the same cinematic, audio-native, multimodal endpoint family.

promptStructure.title: Prompting Seedance 2.0 Fast
promptStructure.quote: [Shot goal] + [subject] + [one clear action beat] + [camera language] + [one sound cue] + [duration target].
promptStructure.description: Keep prompts lean. The fast tier is strongest when each render checks one decision: timing, framing, subject continuity, or audio rhythm.
steps: Name the single decision you want to validate
Add one sound cue if rhythm matters
Escalate winning drafts into standard Seedance 2.0

promptingTip: Tip: keep the same Seedance grammar as standard, but use Fast to answer one question per run: does the text brief work, does the start/end transition land, do the references hold continuity, or is the timing right?

tips.title: Production notes
items: Use Fast for speed, then switch to standard Seedance 2.0 for premium finals.
Use the optional end image in i2v when you want a guided last frame during draft passes.
Keep prompts short and tactical: one beat, one move, one reason to compare.
Use the compare hub to judge Seedance Fast against Veo Fast and LTX Fast, not against every model at once.
Generate shows the active route and the final quote before you submit.

prompts.title: Story beat draft
prompts.prompt: One-shot concept pass for a product reveal, slow push-in, single hero motion beat, soft room tone, designed to validate pacing and framing rather than final polish.

prompts.title: A/B motion test
prompts.prompt: Same product on the same table. Version A uses a locked camera and hand reach. Version B uses a short arc move. Keep lighting identical so only motion changes.

prompts.title: Fast social ad loop
prompts.prompt: Six-second draft of a skincare bottle in a bright bathroom, one clean reveal, one cap twist, one ambient water cue, vertical framing.

faqs.a: Seedance 2.0 Fast is the quicker iteration tier inside the Seedance 2 family, designed for faster draft passes before you move winning directions into standard Seedance 2.0.

faqs.q: Does Seedance 2.0 Fast keep text-to-video, image-to-video, and reference-to-video?
faqs.a: Yes. Seedance 2.0 Fast keeps the same core workflow family as standard Seedance 2.0: text-to-video, image-to-video with an optional end frame, and multimodal reference-to-video.

faqs.q: How many references does Seedance 2.0 Fast accept?
faqs.a: Fast supports up to 9 image references, 3 video references, and 3 audio references, with 12 total files across the full ref2v run.

faqs.q: Does Seedance 2.0 Fast image-to-video support an end frame?
faqs.a: Yes. The image-to-video workflow supports a start image plus an optional end image when you want to guide the last frame in draft passes.

faqs.q: How long can Seedance 2.0 Fast runs be?
faqs.a: Fal lists auto duration or explicit runs from 4 to 15 seconds for the current Seedance 2.0 Fast routes.

faqs.q: When should I use Seedance 2.0 Fast instead of standard Seedance 2.0?
faqs.a: Use Fast when the goal is iteration speed, shot planning, timing checks, and quick comparison passes. Move to standard Seedance 2.0 when you want the most polished final take.

custom.heroTrustLine: Use Fast to decide quickly, then move selected shots into standard Seedance 2.0
heroHighlights: Optimized fast variant||Fal lists Fast as the optimized route inside the Seedance 2 endpoint family
Same workflow family||Text-to-video, image-to-video, and reference-to-video stay available in the same Seedance 2 stack
Native audio plus multimodal references||Fast still sits inside the same audio-native and multimodal Seedance family
Comparison-ready||Pairs naturally against Veo 3.1 Fast and LTX 2.3 Fast
Built for escalation||Move successful drafts into standard Seedance 2.0 for polished finals
custom.specNote: Treat Fast as the quick-iteration version in the Seedance 2 family.

specSections.title: What Fast is for
specSections.intro: Fal positions Fast as the quicker route inside the Seedance 2 family.
items: Creative iteration with shorter feedback loops

specSections.title: Where it fits
specSections.intro: Fast keeps the same core mode coverage and format envelope.
items: Fast draft -> compare -> standard Seedance 2.0 final
Useful against Veo 3.1 Fast and LTX 2.3 Fast
480p or 720p with auto or 4-15s duration control
Best when one prompt tests one decision
Audio-aware workflows stay part of the Seedance family

custom.promptingIntro: Seedance 2.0 Fast works best when each run tests one decision. Keep the brief in Subject -> Action -> Camera -> Style order, then choose the fastest route: text, start/end image, or references.
promptingGlobalPrinciples: Change one variable per run so comparisons stay readable.
Keep the same references when testing camera, timing, or audio variants.
Use start/end image tests to validate transitions, not to rewrite the full scene.
Give every reference one job and trim extra style adjectives.
Escalate winning drafts into standard Seedance 2.0 for final polish.
promptingEngineWhy: Fast is strongest for prompt-only concept checks before expensive finals.
Start/end image tests are useful for landing the last frame without over-directing the middle.
Reference runs are best for continuity A/B checks, not for maximum complexity.
Short audio cues make side-by-side comparisons easier to judge.

promptingTabNotes.structured: Start/End = test the transition and landing frame.
promptingTabNotes.pro: References = test continuity with the same source pack.
promptingTabNotes.storyboard: Compare = package the winner for standard Seedance 2.0.

promptingTabs.label: Text
promptingTabs.title: Text-to-video test prompt
promptingTabs.description: Use when you want a fast read on framing, motion, or mood from language alone.
promptingTabs.copy:
Subject:
[Who/what appears]

Action:
[One visible beat only]

Camera:
[One short move]

Style:
[Lighting + palette]

Goal:
[What this run is proving: timing / framing / motion / audio]

Rule:
Do not combine multiple decisions in the same Fast run.

promptingTabs.label: Start/End
promptingTabs.title: Image-to-video transition test
promptingTabs.description: Use when you need to validate how a start frame lands on an end frame.
promptingTabs.copy:
Start image:
[What is fixed from frame 1]

End image (optional):
[What the last frame should land on]

Motion path:
[How the subject travels between the two]

Camera:
[One move only]

What should stay identical across versions:
[Lighting / wardrobe / background / pace]

Use this when the question is transition quality, not final polish.

promptingTabs.label: References
promptingTabs.title: Reference continuity test
promptingTabs.description: Use when you are checking whether the same reference pack holds together across variants.

promptingTabs.label: Compare
promptingTabs.title: Comparison / handoff prompt
promptingTabs.description: Use this when Fast is selecting a winner before the standard tier.
promptingTabs.copy:
What stays fixed across A/B:
[Subject / refs / setting / duration / ratio]

Variant A:
[Camera / pacing / sound choice]

Variant B:
[One changed variable only]

Winner criteria:
[What counts as success]

If approved, move into standard Seedance 2.0 with:
[Extra polish, richer sound design, fuller shot plan]

strengths: Good for shot planning and pacing checks
Natural stepping stone into standard Seedance 2.0
boundaries: Do not judge final polish from Fast alone.
Complex prompts make Fast harder to compare.
Treat it as a draft tier, not a replacement for the standard tier.
Use the final quote and route details inside Generate before scaling winners.
custom.galleryIntro: Review the kinds of draft passes and comparison prompts that make Seedance 2.0 Fast useful in a real production workflow.
